page: Amazon Web Services c6a.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the x86_64 architecture. It features 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores driven by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, translating to a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance utilizes the AWS Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong performance characteristics, particularly in multi-core processing and specialized compute tasks. The instance achieves top 25% results in stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores. Cryptographic capabilities are supported by hardware-accelerated instructions like AES, VAES, and SHA_NI, yielding strong results in PassMark encryption tests. Memory subsystem performance is strong for small-block cached reads, though large-block uncached reads and writes fall into the average tier.

Given its compute-heavy profile and 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, the c6a.16xlarge is well-suited for high-throughput workloads such as software compilation, image processing, and database operations, as evidenced by its strong PassMark database and Redis SET performance. However, the lack of local NVMe storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which may introduce latency tradeoffs for I/O-intensive applications. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio also makes it less suitable for memory-bound workloads compared to memory-optimized alternatives.
description: Amazon Web Services c6a.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed by the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory, providing a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but delivers a 25 Gbps baseline network speed. Performance benchmarks place this server in the top 25% for multi-core processing, software compilation, and database operations. It also demonstrates strong cryptographic execution via hardware-accelerated AES and SHA instructions. The c6a.16xlarge is highly efficient for compute-intensive workloads, including software compilation, ray tracing, and high-throughput database operations, though it requires network-attached storage for data persistence.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6a.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs, an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor, and 128.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, designed for high-throughput multi-core workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6a.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 64 vCPUs, 128.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and a 25 Gbps baseline network speed.
tagline: A high-density compute instance featuring sixty-four virtual cores and dedicated AMD EPYC processing for demanding multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 64-vCPU AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz
Equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory at a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Delivers 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the Nitro hypervisor
Strong benchmark performance in multi-core processing and software compilation
Optimized for compute-heavy workloads, database operations, and cryptography
Requires network-attached storage due to the absence of local disks
